lessons.lesson: From Prompt Engineering to Context Engineering
lessons.core_mechanism: capability expansion by moving from one-shot prompts to controlled context loading
lessons.physical_analogy: a tiny workbench that first uses only spoken instructions, then starts pulling the right tools onto the table before each task
lessons.label_fusion: prompt, context, and task labels are embedded on bench cards and the loading chute
lessons.composition_intent: left-to-right progression from one-shot output to scoped context-assisted work
lessons.source_excerpt: Harness engineering existed before the term was coined. Early agents worked with tiny context windows, so prompt-only execution was not enough. Teams started asking how to recycle small memory space to do more with less, which pushed the industry from prompt engineering toward context engineering.

lessons.lesson: Tool Calling, MCP, and RAG as Context Controls
lessons.core_mechanism: external capability routing so the agent reads only relevant repo state, vendor features, and retrieved knowledge
lessons.physical_analogy: a control room that sends runners to the file archive, vendor desk, or knowledge library instead of piling everything on one operator table
lessons.label_fusion: tool, MCP, and RAG labels are attached to runners and station counters
lessons.composition_intent: hub-and-spoke capability map with the agent in the center and three context channels branching outward
lessons.source_excerpt: Tool calling, MCP, and RAG were used to manage the context window more efficiently. Tool calling explored only the relevant files, MCP added vendor features, and RAG connected external databases for on-demand data.

lessons.lesson: The Summarization Trap in Long-Running Tasks
lessons.core_mechanism: lossy context compression causing the agent to forget unfinished work and misreport completion
lessons.physical_analogy: a project manager who keeps rewriting a giant checklist into shorter summaries until critical unchecked boxes disappear
lessons.label_fusion: summary, dropped detail, and false-done labels sit directly on checklist cards and red warning arrows
lessons.composition_intent: downward compression loop that shrinks memory and then branches into wrong assumptions
lessons.source_excerpt: As long tasks ran, agents would summarize context to shrink it and continue. The task then became bound by the agent’s own ability to summarize prior work, which is why tasks were often half completed or incorrectly assumed finished.

lessons.lesson: Harness Engineering as a Controlled Iteration Loop
lessons.core_mechanism: looping the agent through bounded tasks with fresh context, test gates, and progress documentation
lessons.physical_analogy: an assembly line that pulls one card at a time, completes it, checks it, stamps it, and only then moves to the next card
lessons.label_fusion: requirements, task card, test, and document labels are embedded on conveyor stations
lessons.composition_intent: circular execution loop with a visible reset point between iterations
lessons.source_excerpt: Harness engineering stepped one layer above context engineering by looping the agent in a strict rule set. Each iteration got a fresh prompt and fresh context, then implemented, tested, and documented one task until the whole plan was finished.

lessons.lesson: How Prompt, Context, and Harness Layers Fit Together
lessons.core_mechanism: stacked control architecture where prompt defines identity, context defines working set, and harness defines lifecycle orchestration
lessons.physical_analogy: a three-layer stage setup where the script guides the actor, stagehands bring props, and the director controls scene order
lessons.label_fusion: prompt, context, and harness labels are embedded on stage script, prop cart, and director booth
lessons.composition_intent: layered stack showing each control surface and how the top layer uses the lower two
lessons.source_excerpt: Harness engineering does not deprecate context engineering or prompt engineering. It leverages both. Prompt shapes the coding agent persona, context loads the relevant information, and harnessing orchestrates them through a stepwise environment.

lessons.lesson: From Cloud Agents to Autonomous Production Cycles
lessons.core_mechanism: background automation that chains agent runs, cloud execution, notifications, and recurring checks into a repeatable production loop
lessons.physical_analogy: a night-shift operations center that receives requests, dispatches workers, checks daily changes, and raises a ticket only when a real update is ready
lessons.label_fusion: cloud agent, PR, Slack, and daily check labels sit on dispatch boards and event rails
lessons.composition_intent: closed production cycle from request trigger to autonomous execution to PR feedback to scheduled reruns
lessons.source_excerpt: The video shows local agents, cloud agents, Slack-triggered requests, and daily automated checks that keep a site updated. This demonstrates harnessing as a production cycle, not just a chat-session trick.
